lede: A look at October 29 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jon Abrahams and Kelly Smith, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: China
events.desc: China announces the end of its one-child policy after 35 years.

events.title: Hurricane Sandy
events.desc: Hurricane Sandy hits the east coast of the United States, killing hundreds, while leaving nearly $70 billion in damages and causing major power outages.

events.title: 1999 Odisha cyclone
events.desc: A large cyclone devastates Odisha, India.

events.title: Galileo (spacecraft)
events.desc: The American Galileo spacecraft makes its closest approach to 951 Gaspra, becoming the first probe to visit an asteroid.

events.title: Margaret Thatcher
events.desc: British Prime Minister Margaret Thatcher opens the last stretch of the M25 motorway.

events.title: Computer
events.desc: The first-ever computer-to-computer link is established on ARPANET, the precursor to the Internet.

events.title: Montreal
events.desc: Montreal's World Fair, Expo 67, closes with over 50 million visitors.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I : the Ottoman Empire enters the war on the side of the Central Powers.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Battle of Wauhatchie: Forces under Union General Ulysses S. Grant repel a Confederate attack led by General James Longstreet in one of the few night battles of the war, protecting the Union's recently opened supply line into Chattanooga, Tennessee.

events.title: Geneva
events.desc: Eighteen countries meet in Geneva and agree to form the International Red Cross.

births.name: Joseph Goebbels
births.line: German lawyer and politician, Chancellor of Nazi Germany

births.name: Abdullah Gül
births.line: Turkish academic and politician, 11th President of Turkey

births.name: Ellen Johnson Sirleaf
births.line: Liberian politician, President of Liberia, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Dan Castellaneta
births.line: American actor, voice artist, comedian, singer and producer

deaths.text: J. Bernlef, Dutch author, poet, and songwriter
Anton LaVey, American occultist, founded the Church of Satan
Jean Le Rond d'Alembert, French mathematician, physicist, and philosopher
